skill.name: maxhub-zhihu
skill.description: Query Zhihu (Chinese Q&A platform) for user profiles, search results, columns, Q&A, trending topics, and comments through the MaxHub API.
skill.summary: Zhihu data assistant for retrieving user info, search results, trending topics, column articles, Q&A content, and comments via the MaxHub API.
skill.author: XieWxx

security_audit.summary: All 286 static findings are either false positives (markdown code fences misidentified as backtick execution, markdown table formatting as weak cryptography, documentation tables as system reconnaissance) or legitimate/declared behaviors (env_access for the documented MAXHUB_API_KEY, network calls to the declared API endpoint at aconfig.cn). The heuristic critical finding for 'dangerous combination' was triggered by the aggregation of false-positive patterns. The skill transparently declares all dependencies in SKILL.md metadata. No prompt injection, obfuscation, or malicious intent detected.

medium_findings.title: Environment variable access for API authentication
medium_findings.description: The skill reads MAXHUB_API_KEY from the environment for API authorization. This is expected and declared in SKILL.md metadata with sensitive:true flag. The key is used only as an Authorization: Bearer header to the documented API endpoint. No evidence of exfiltration or misuse.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Confirmed legitimate use - the env access is declared, documented, and used only for its stated purpose of API authentication.

medium_findings.title: Network calls to external API service
medium_findings.description: The skill makes HTTP requests to https://www.aconfig.cn for Zhihu data retrieval. This is the core function of the skill and is fully declared in SKILL.md metadata and documentation. The endpoint is a legitimate API gateway service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Confirmed legitimate use - network calls are the core function, transparently declared with endpoint documentation.

low_findings.title: Markdown code fences detected as shell execution (false positive)
low_findings.description: The static analyzer identified backtick patterns in markdown files as Ruby/shell backtick execution. These are actually markdown code block delimiters (```bash) enclosing curl command examples for documentation purposes. No actual command execution occurs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Very high confidence this is a false positive - code block formatting is visually distinct from shell backtick execution. All instances are inside markdown code fences.

low_findings.title: Markdown table formatting detected as weak crypto (false positive)
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged pipe and dash characters in markdown tables as 'weak cryptographic algorithm'. These are standard markdown table syntax for API parameter documentation. No actual cryptographic operations exist in the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Very high confidence false positive - the detected 'algorithm' patterns are markdown table delimiters, not crypto implementations.

low_findings.title: Markdown table formatting detected as system reconnaissance (false positive)
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged parameter descriptions like 'article_id' in API documentation as system reconnaissance. These are standard API parameter documentation, not reconnaissance commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: High confidence false positive - detected patterns are API documentation parameters, not system reconnaissance.

low_findings.title: Heuristic dangerous combination alert (false positive)
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged the co-occurrence of network, external_commands, and env_access patterns as a 'dangerous combination'. Each individual pattern is either a false positive (external_commands) or legitimate declared behavior (env_access, network). No actual malicious chain of operations exists.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: High confidence false positive - the heuristic aggregated three patterns where two are legitimate and one is a scanner error.

content.user_title: Query Zhihu data for user profiles, trending topics, and content analysis
content.value_statement: Accessing Zhihu (Chinese Q&A platform) data through standard APIs requires complex integration work. This skill provides a ready-to-use interface to query user profiles, search results, trending topics, column articles, and comments through the MaxHub API with a single API key.
actual_capabilities: Retrieve user profiles, followers, followees, and subscription data from Zhihu
Search Zhihu content including articles, answers, columns, videos, ebooks, and scholarly papers
Fetch trending topics and hot lists from Zhihu with pagination support
Query column articles, article details, comments, and sub-comments
Support AI-powered search results and search suggestions
Classify queries into browse, analyze, or compare modes for structured output
limitations: Requires a valid MaxHub API key obtained from aconfig.cn
Data accuracy depends on the third-party API provider, not official Zhihu APIs
Rate limits may apply (HTTP 429) when making too many requests in short time
Only covers data accessible through the MaxHub API gateway, not all Zhihu features

use_cases.title: Professional knowledge research
use_cases.description: Research teams can collect domain knowledge from Zhihu by searching for expert answers and analyzing respondent backgrounds. Generate structured knowledge summaries from high-quality Q&A content.

use_cases.title: Brand sentiment monitoring
use_cases.description: Brand teams can monitor Zhihu discussions about their products or industry. Search brand keywords, analyze comment sentiment, and generate reputation reports.

use_cases.title: Trending topic tracking
use_cases.description: Content teams can discover high-interest topics from Zhihu hot lists, analyze trend patterns, and generate content strategy suggestions based on community engagement data.

prompt_templates.title: Basic user lookup
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for a Zhihu user named [username] and summarize their profile, recent articles, and follower count.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick user information lookup for social media analysis

prompt_templates.title: Trending topics overview
prompt_templates.prompt: Get the current trending topics from Zhihu hot list. Show the top 10 items with their engagement metrics.

prompt_templates.title: Topic deep analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze Zhihu content about [topic]. Search for relevant articles and answers, summarize key viewpoints, identify top contributors, and highlight contrasting opinions.
prompt_templates.scenario: Comprehensive topic research with multiple data points

prompt_templates.title: Compare user influence
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare Zhihu users [user1] and [user2]. Show their follower counts, article counts, column subscriptions, and interaction levels side by side.

output_examples.input: What are the trending topics on Zhihu today?
output_examples.output:
Here are the top 10 trending topics on Zhihu today:

1. **AI Development Trends** - 12.3M views, 2,456 answers
   Hot discussion around recent AI regulatory policies

2. **New Energy Vehicles** - 8.7M views, 1,892 answers
   Price competition among major EV manufacturers

3. **Remote Work Impact** - 5.2M views, 1,234 answers
   Analysis of productivity changes in tech companies

4. ... (continues to top 10)

Key findings: Technology and energy topics dominate today's trending list.

output_examples.input: Find articles written by user 'zhangwei' and analyze their content focus.
output_examples.output:
I found user **Zhang Wei** - a verified technology columnist with 125,000 followers.

**Recent articles (5):**
1. "The Future of Machine Learning" - 45,000 reads, 892 likes
2. "Understanding Transformer Architecture" - 32,000 reads, 654 likes
3. "AI Ethics in Practice" - 28,000 reads, 523 likes
4. "Python for Data Analysis" - 22,000 reads, 412 likes
5. "Cloud Computing Trends 2025" - 18,000 reads, 389 likes

**Content focus:** Machine learning and AI applications (60%), programming tutorials (25%), industry analysis (15%).

best_practices: Start with a simple query to verify the API key is working before running complex multi-endpoint analyses
Use the browse mode for quick lookups and analyze mode for deeper research to get appropriately structured results
Include specific keywords in Chinese and English for better search accuracy on bilingual content
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to scrape or access Zhihu directly - this skill only works through the declared MaxHub API
Do not share the MAXHUB_API_KEY value in conversation output or include it in shared prompts
Do not make excessive parallel requests - respect rate limits by waiting between query batches

faq.question: Do I need an API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. You need a MaxHub API key from aconfig.cn. The key is stored as the MAXHUB_API_KEY environment variable and used for API authentication.

faq.question: Does this skill access Zhihu directly or through a third party?
faq.answer: This skill accesses Zhihu data through the MaxHub API gateway (aconfig.cn), not through official Zhihu APIs. Data is provided by a third-party service.

faq.question: What languages does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill supports both Chinese and English. It detects the user's language from the query and responds in the same language.

faq.question: What data can I retrieve with this skill?
faq.answer: You can retrieve user profiles, search results, trending topics, column articles, Q&A content, comments, AI search results, and video lists.

faq.question: Is my API key safe when using this skill?
faq.answer: The SKILL.md explicitly instructs the AI to keep API key values out of output. The key is only used in the Authorization header for API calls.

faq.question: What happens if I get a 429 error?
faq.answer: A 429 error means you have exceeded the rate limit. Wait before making more requests. The skill handles common errors like 403 (skip and note), 502 (retry once), and empty results.
